description_long: This object is an antique flintlock pistol, characterized by a long, cylindrical metal barrel and a curved wooden grip. The metal components, including the barrel, trigger, and hammer, exhibit a weathered, darkened patina, suggesting age and frequent handling. The grip is made of wood, smoothly contoured and slightly bulbous at the end, with visible grain and some surface wear. The trigger guard is a simple, thin metal loop, and the hammer mechanism is exposed, typical of early firearm designs. There are no visible modern features such as a slide or magazine, and the overall construction is robust but utilitarian. The finish is matte and rough, with no apparent engravings or decorative markings.
description: An antique flintlock pistol with a long metal barrel and a curved wooden grip, featuring a weathered, matte finish and exposed hammer mechanism.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, the curved wooden grip, and the exposed hammer and trigger guard.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular barrel opening and the alignment of the barrel with the hammer mechanism above.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the grip's curvature, the trigger guard, and the length of the barrel.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the grip and the top of the barrel, with the hammer mechanism partially visible.
attribute_grip_design: The grip is smoothly contoured and made of dark, aged wood, with a rounded, bulbous end that fits comfortably in the palm. The surface is mostly smooth but shows signs of wear and subtle grain patterns, lacking any checkering or stippling. There are no visible logos, finger grooves, or embellishments, giving it a utilitarian and classic appearance.
attribute_trigger_guard_style: The trigger guard is a thin, simple metal loop with a gentle curve, closely following the contour of the grip. It is unadorned, with no undercuts, squared edges, or integrated accessories, and appears to be slightly worn, matching the aged look of the rest of the pistol.
attribute_barrel_type: The barrel is long, round, and smooth, with a uniform cylindrical profile and no visible threading, porting, or fluting. It has a dark, matte finish with some surface wear and patina, and lacks any compensators or external attachments.
attribute_slide_markings: There are no visible markings, engravings, or inscriptions on the barrel or other metal parts. The surface is plain and unadorned, with only natural wear and patina visible.
attribute_magazine_base_shape: There is no visible magazine base or floorplate, as this is a single-shot flintlock pistol. The grip ends in a rounded, bulbous wooden pommel with no extensions, bevels, or finger rests.
